title: AP Calculus: Power Rule — Differentiate in Seconds
description:
The Power Rule is the most-used differentiation rule on the AP Calculus exam. Once you master this, you can differentiate most polynomial functions instantly.

📐 Topic: Derivatives — Power Rule (AP Calculus AB/BC)
⏱️ Difficulty: Easy
📝 AP Exam: FRQ Part A / MCQ

In this video:
✅ State the Power Rule formula
✅ Apply it to a polynomial function
✅ Find the value of the derivative at a point
✅ Common mistakes to avoid

🔗 Full AP Calculus textbook: https://mathhub.io/us/textbook/ap-calculus/

Timestamps:
0:00 The Power Rule formula
0:20 Example: differentiate f(x) = 4x³ − 2x² + 5x − 3
0:50 Apply the rule term by term
1:20 Evaluate f'(2)
1:50 Answer and check
2:00 Key takeaway

#APCalculus #PowerRule #Derivatives #APCalc #CollegeBoard #Calculus #MathTutorial #APExam

slides.heading: Apply the Power Rule term by term

slides.explanation: Key rules: (1) Power Rule brings the exponent down and reduces it by 1. (2) Constants vanish. (3) Linear terms become constants. Apply term by term — it's that simple!
